programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Swim Fit
programs.description: The course name says it all—Swim Fit. In this activity, you’ll not only get instruction on technique and stroke improvement, but you’ll also get the opportunity to perform interval drills to increase your swimming performance and endurance.Prerequisite: Must be able to swim two continuous lengths of the pool or 50 metres.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 (Girls)
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4. This activity is for all girls.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4 (Girls)
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. This activity is for all girls.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Basics
programs.description: Combining influences of lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip hop and modern dance this activity is the perfect for beginners. Learn basic positions, body awareness and proper technique through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Basics with Caregiver
programs.description: Combining influences of lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip hop and modern dance this activity is the perfect for beginners. Learn basic positions, body awareness and proper technique through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: Creative Movement
programs.description: Express yourself creatively with dance. Pairing basic movements with imagination, learn dance elements like body awareness and rhythm through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Bronze
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this first level of the Challenge Swim program you will learn backstroke starts and somersaults for flip turns while continuing to work on interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 200-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Small cooks with big imaginations will have the chance to take over the kitchen. Starting with cooking basics, you will learn about healthy foods and the importance of kitchen safety, then through hands-on practice you will learn how to mix and measure, use cool kitchen tools and follow recipes that explore fun flavours that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8

To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.description: Want to liven up the kitchen table? Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cardio: Kickboxing
programs.description: Calling all fitness warriors. This high-intensity cardio workout that incorporates non-contact punches, kicks and athletic moves will help you improve your strength, balance and coordination.

programs.name: Computers
programs.description: Designed for first-time users or those with some knowledge, this introduction to computers focuses on how to use a device with confidence. Learn basic computer skills like navigation as well as how to create an email, access the web, create documents and more.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects.

programs.name: Dance: Creative Movement With Caregiver
programs.description: Express yourself creatively with dance. Pairing basic movements with imagination, learn dance elements like body awareness and rhythm through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Music: Drumming - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the drum with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn grip, stroke, rhythm and hand and foot coordination.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Singing - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal singing instruction with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn the importance of proper posture and warm-ups as well as voice projection.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Dance: Chinese
programs.description: Chinese dances encompassing a diverse range of styles consisting of many modern and traditional dance genres. Some well-known examples include the Dragon Dance, Lion Dance, Fan Dance, and the various ethnic dances of China's minority groups. Learn traditional dance steps, footwork and hand movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Breakdance
programs.description: This athletic dance combines elements of hip-hop with acrobatics. Learn basic moves including top rock, down rock, power moves and freezes through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Chinese
programs.description: Chinese dances encompassing a diverse range of styles consisting of many modern and traditional dance genres. Some well-known examples include the Dragon Dance, Lion Dance, Fan Dance, and the various ethnic dances of China's minority groups. Learn traditional dance steps, footwork and hand movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Play
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will have an opportunity for sensory exploration through arts, dance, and music.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Zumba®
programs.description: Latin dance and music inspire this fun and energetic cardio workout. Using high and low-impact steps and aerobic movements, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Creative Movement
programs.description: Express yourself creatively with dance. Pairing basic movements with imagination, learn dance elements like body awareness and rhythm through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Fusion with Caregiver
programs.description: Experience a mix of dance styles including lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip-hop, and modern dance. Learn basic steps that focus on flexibility, strength and endurance through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Gymnastics with Caregiver
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on fun and safety, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: HDA Ball Hockey
programs.description: HDA Ball Hockey is a partnership program with the Hockey Diversity Alliance that promotes hockey opportunities for everyone. Instructors lead participants through activities and drills designed to support skill development. All classes are tailored to the skill levels of participants registered in that session.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: Chinese dances encompassing a diverse range of styles consisting of many modern and traditional dance genres. Some well-known examples include the Dragon Dance, Lion Dance, Fan Dance, and the various ethnic dances of China's minority groups. Learn traditional dance steps, footwork and hand movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Dance: Hawaiian
programs.description: Hawaiian dance, known as Hula, is all about storytelling. Focusing on etiquette, attire and musical style, you will learn hip and footwork movements with flowing hands through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.
